campaigns.ask: Engage with management and board on strategic direction, potential extraordinary corporate transactions, asset sales, board changes, and governance matters.

campaigns.ask: Remove entrenched board, call special meeting, and pursue a banker-led dual-path strategic process (sale or partnership).
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest shareholder, is waging a proxy fight to overturn a 70% supermajority bylaw he calls entrenchment, after the company dropped its own lawsuit to avoid discovery. He is pushing for an independent, competitive sale or partnership process to maximize shareholder value.

campaigns.ask: Replace board / change strategy to secure maximum shareholder value, focusing on contract risk management and execution

He and a slate of director nominees are soliciting shareholder support to regain control and ensure contracts are managed as assets rather than liabilit

campaigns.ask: Acquire the company via a merger with a Tang Capital affiliate.
campaigns.summary: Tang Capital, holding a 6.8% stake, submitted a merger proposal to Nano Dimension on June 30, 2026, outlining terms to acquire the company subject to limited confirmatory diligence. The activist intends to engage in negotiations with the issuer to pursue the transaction.

campaigns.ask: Board representation / equity-based compensation arrangement for board service

campaigns.ask: Remove and replace board members, declassify the board, require shareholder approval for poison pill and major transactions, and hold an extraordinary general meeting.

campaigns.company: BNY Mellon Municipal Bond Infrastructure Fund, Inc.

The Fund's Board unanimously opposes both the declassification proposal and Saba's director nominee, urging stockholders to vote against them.

campaigns.ask: Acquire all outstanding shares not already owned at $5.25 per share in cash

The group has filed a definitive proxy statement and is urging shareholders to support the call for a special meeting.

campaigns.ask: Allegation: systematic 'license renting' scheme at ~20% of skilled nursing facilities, constituting fraud against Medicare/Medicaid and inflating margins/growth.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters alleges Ensign rents the licenses of absent Administrators at roughly 20% of its SNFs, enabling facilities to bill Medicare/Medicaid despite lacking legally required on-site licensed management. It estimates compliance would cut 2027 EBIT ~35% versus consensus and expose the company to multi-billion-dollar False Claims Act sanctions.

campaigns.ask: Allegation that T1 Energy is effectively a shell company secretly controlled by Trina Solar, supported by whistleblower documents including emails, ERP systems, and account records.
campaigns.summary: Fuzzy Panda's second installment in its whistleblower series on T1 Energy alleges the company is essentially a front for Trina Solar, with a whistleblower providing internal emails, ERP data, and account records purportedly confirming Trina's covert control.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer (Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and affiliates)
campaigns.ask: Call a Special Meeting to elect a new independent Board majority committed to a full-value strategic review/sale process.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest shareholder, is soliciting shareholders to call a Special Meeting to replace the unelected board majority with six independent directors who would run a rigorous sales process, accusing the current board of avoiding accountability and blanket-rejecting a sale without testing the market.

campaigns.ask: Two board seats (Jens Ismar and Paul Cornell), vote against poison pill ratification and equity incentive plan
campaigns.summary: Diana Shipping, Genco's largest shareholder, is running a proxy fight to elect two independent directors to Genco's board and defeat ratification of Genco's poison pill and equity incentive plan, citing board entrenchment and excessive compensation. ISS has recommended shareholders vote against both governance proposals.

campaigns.ask: Alleges T1 Energy misled investors by secretly continuing to purchase solar cells from Trina Solar, a designated Chinese Military Company, despite claiming it had stopped.
campaigns.summary: A whistleblower provided 26 invoices showing T1 Energy continued buying solar cells from Trina Solar after executives told investors the relationship had ended. Fuzzy Panda frames this as the first part of a whistleblower series on the company.

campaigns.campaigner: Al Shams Investments LTD (ASIL) / Wafic Rida Said

The campaign follows a public letter to the board's outside directors and an amended 13D filing, signaling dissatisfaction with the company's current governance or strategic direction.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Solicit proxies to call a special meeting of shareholders.
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer and affiliated parties are soliciting shareholder proxies and agent designations to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, anticipated to be held around June 30, 2026.

campaigns.ask: Allegation: detailed methodology and data analysis supporting a short thesis on Ensign Group's business practices
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook published a methodology piece detailing the data, reporting process, and analysis behind its short investigation into Ensign Group, a skilled nursing facility operator. The report outlines the analytical framework used to support allegations regarding the company's operations.

campaigns.ask: Alleges Ensign's nursing home empire is built on fatal neglect, patient harm, and preventable deaths.
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook published a short report accusing Ensign Group of systemic neglect across its nursing home operations, tying the company's growth model to patient harm and preventable fatalities.

campaigns.ask: Alleges Pirelli has undisclosed deep ties to Russia's military-industrial complex, derives ~10% of profits from Russia, and may be violating EU sanctions — posing a Western national security threat.
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research alleges Pirelli is the only Western tire maker still manufacturing in Russia, with ~10% of profits from Russian operations that support a state-owned military tire plant producing tires for nuclear-warhead launchers and vehicles used in the Ukraine invasion. The report also cites apparent EU sanctions violations via a dealer in Russian-occupied Donetsk and Kremlin-linked ownership

campaigns.ask: Alleges overvaluation of assets in evergreen private equity funds, now evidenced by surging redemption requests forcing the firm to cap withdrawals.
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research highlights that Partners Group is capping withdrawals at 5% of NAV per quarter after requests surged to 9.8%, causing an 18% stock drop. This follows Grizzly's April 2026 report detailing severe concerns about the firm's valuation marks in its evergreen funds.

campaigns.ask: Short report alleging Ethos is a low-quality lead-generation business with a narrow moat, heavy ad spend, and consumer misconduct complaints rather than a transformative tech-driven insurance platform.
campaigns.summary: The Bear Cave alleges Ethos Technologies (NASDAQ: LIFE) is essentially a life insurance lead-generation business masquerading as a tech platform, spending heavily on advertising and facing regulator-substantiated consumer complaints of agent misconduct. The report questions the company's premium valuation and business quality.

campaigns.ask: Overvaluation - stock has soared on speculative AI mania despite being a niche industrial memory chipmaker not a meaningful AI beneficiary.
campaigns.summary: Kerrisdale Capital is short Everspin Technologies, alleging the stock has surged 300% on misplaced AI hype even though its primary end markets are industrial and casino gaming. Kerrisdale sees fair value at $14 per share, representing ~60% downside, as the company's revenue has stagnated around $50-65 million for years.

campaigns.ask: Alleges controlling shareholder Hans Georg Näder's €1.5B margin loan against his 81% stake, aggressive accounting, and Russia military-linked revenue create a massive overhang and overvaluation risk for minority sharehol
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research alleges Ottobock's majority owner Hans Georg Näder has pledged all his shares in a ~€1.5B PIK margin loan maturing in 2030, creating a forced-sale overhang on an illiquid stock, while the company inflates earnings via aggressive R&D capitalization and non-consolidation of Russia branches. The report also flags that ~35% of net income derives from Russia sales—potentially servicing

campaigns.ask: Alleges hidden Chinese ties will disqualify T1 Energy from US tax credits it has already claimed, rendering it unprofitable, and predicts imminent accounting restatements.
campaigns.summary: Fuzzy Panda's short report alleges T1 Energy, an American solar module maker, has undisclosed Chinese connections that will invalidate its eligibility for US tax credits, leading to restatements and significant financial harm.

campaigns.ask: Alleges suspicious financial activity involving billions in backlog funneled through a single intermediary in Indonesia, with a mystery payment raising red flags about the company's revenue recognition and financial inte
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook's short report on CSG alleges that a mystery payment may explain billions in backlog channeled through a single suspicious Indonesian intermediary, raising concerns about potential accounting irregularities and the sustainability of the company's reported backlog.

campaigns.ask: Allegation: Kinsale's industry-leading margins are unsustainable, driven by overpriced, exclusion-heavy policies sold to unsophisticated small-business customers in the lightly regulated E&S market, with a pattern of cla
campaigns.summary: The Bear Cave alleges Kinsale's premium economics rely on charging high premiums for watered-down coverage with expanding exclusions, resulting in a 60% retention rate and regulatory complaints alleging overcharges and ineffective insurance. The report concludes the business is low quality and unsustainable.

campaigns.ask: Allegation that the largest military IPO in European history is fundamentally flawed/combusting
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook published a short report on CSG, alleging significant problems with what it describes as the largest military IPO in European history, using the metaphor of 'combusting' to signal serious operational or financial deficiencies. The report implies the company's business is deteriorating or was misrepresented to investors.

campaigns.ask: Allegation of improper valuation/accounting — marking up Russian-exposed investments despite impairment from state seizure of portfolio asset
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research alleges Partners Group marked up its Master Fund's stake in Ciddan (which holds a 49.78% interest in Nidda Topco, owner of Russian pharmaceutical manufacturer Nizhpharm) despite the Russian state transfer of Nizhpharm constituting a direct impairment event. The short firm questions whether the valuation markup is supportable given the seizure, implying either improper valuation or

campaigns.ask: Alleges Partners Group severely mismarks valuations in its evergreen funds, with ~40% of investments potentially overstated, threatening fundraising and long-term financial health.
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research alleges Partners Group's Master Fund and other evergreen funds contain numerous valuation irregularities, including a $322M holding with 50M missing shares, aggressive markups on deteriorating businesses, a Russian asset marked up after state seizure, and understated private-credit software exposure. The firm consulted experts, one calling it 'worse than Wirecard,' and sees the mi

campaigns.ask: Alleges Hamilton Lane's private equity business faces murky valuation and return issues that are underappreciated by the market.
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook's short report argues that while investors focus on private credit, Hamilton Lane's private equity portfolio may harbor deeper problems, suggesting valuations and returns are overstated or unsustainable.

campaigns.ask: Short report alleging financial, operational, or overvaluation concerns.
campaigns.summary: Spruce Point published a short report on Zoom Communications, Inc., detailing alleged risks and concerns regarding the company's financial reporting or business trajectory.

campaigns.ask: Alleges SRAD systematically aids and abets illegal gambling operators in banned markets, contradicting management's compliance/KYC claims, with illegal operators estimated at 20-40% of revenues.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short SRAD, citing undercover investigations, source-code analysis, and employee testimony showing the company actively supplies illegal gambling operators across black/grey markets (China, Southeast Asia, Russia) while CEO Carsten Koerl publicly claims rigorous compliance. The report identifies ~50 current/recent illegal-market clients, including organized-crime- and human-traffic

campaigns.ask: Alleges fraudulent transfers, asset misappropriation, and AI hype — questions leadership competence after CEO Toby Neugebauer's abrupt departure.
campaigns.summary: Fuzzy Panda alleges Fermi America engaged in fraudulent transfers and asset misappropriation, and claims the company is overstating its ability to execute on a massive AI data center and power project. The report notes CEO and co-founder Toby Neugebauer's sudden Friday departure, which Fuzzy Panda believes was a firing, leaving remaining executives who lack the experience to deliver on the AI infr

campaigns.ask: Alleges paid stock promotion and overvaluation; unprofitable AI company trading at ~20x revenue with limited proprietary technology.
campaigns.summary: The Bear Cave alleges Rezolve AI paid $1.5 million for a six-month stock promotion campaign subcontracted through YouTube channels and political commentary influencers, signaling management's focus on inflating share price rather than building product. The report also references prior Fuzzy Panda allegations that the company is essentially a 'ChatGPT wrapper' with a CEO 'infamous for fake customer

campaigns.ask: Allegation that Sinch's subsidiary Inteliquent is the largest enabler of scam and robocalls in the Western Hemisphere, faces mounting regulatory scrutiny, and has inadequate compliance — risks undisclosed to investors.
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research alleges that Sinch's $1.14B acquisition Inteliquent is responsible for nearly half of US robocalls and is the single biggest enabler of scam calls in the Western Hemisphere, with regulators closing in. The firm argues Sinch's compliance team is effectively non-existent and that the company may be consciously turning a blind eye to fraudulent users, posing material undisclosed regu

campaigns.ask: Allegation that Meta is secretly behind a $1B Ohio data center facing local community pushback over water supply and noise pollution concerns.
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook alleges Meta is covertly backing a $1B data center project in Ohio that has drawn significant local opposition over environmental and quality-of-life concerns. The report suggests reputational and regulatory risk for Meta from the undisclosed involvement.

campaigns.ask: Allegation of misleading and fraudulent billing practices, deceptive free trials, and harassing telemarketing targeting small businesses
campaigns.summary: The Bear Cave published a short report on Yelp citing hundreds of consumer complaints obtained via FOIA requests alleging a pattern of fraudulent billing, deceptive free ad trials that convert to unexpected charges, and excessive telemarketing despite Do Not Call requests, reinforcing concerns about a broken sales culture at the company.

campaigns.ask: Allegates improper accounting treatment of land reservation fees that Lennar counts as assets despite the obligations coming due
campaigns.summary: Hunterbrook alleges that America's second-largest homebuilder spends billions to reserve land it previously owned and improperly counts these fees as assets, with the bill coming due. The short thesis centers on the accounting treatment of these land option obligations and the future cash burden they represent.

campaigns.ask: Allegation of accounting fraud: SoFi booked a $312M borrowing from JPMorgan as a loan sale, inflating profits and management bonuses, with a restatement likely to erase ~$1B of reported EBITDA and materially lower capita
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short SoFi, alleging the company misclassified a $312M JPMorgan borrowing as a loan sale in Q3 2024, which inflated earnings and executive compensation while shareholders face ~15% annual dilution. The firm believes Utah UCC filings contradict SoFi's accounting and expects a restatement that would also reduce ~$1B of previously reported EBITDA and materially lower capital ratios.

campaigns.ask: Short report alleging accounting and financial concerns, forecasting downside risk.
campaigns.summary: Spruce Point published a short report on Bunge Global SA, highlighting potential accounting and financial issues that the firm believes are not fully reflected in the company's current valuation.

campaigns.ask: Allegation that Accor has ties to child trafficking through associations with Jeffrey Epstein and Ukrainian orphans, prompting the company to launch an internal investigation.
campaigns.summary: Grizzly Research published a report alleging Accor's connections to child trafficking via Epstein and Ukrainian orphans, which garnered widespread media coverage from Bloomberg, Reuters, FT, and others. Accor responded by announcing a detailed internal investigation with an external firm to verify the claims.

campaigns.ask: Allegation that SoFi's 2025 Adjusted EBITDA is inflated by ~90% and that executives have extracted significant proceeds via prepaid variable forward contracts.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short SoFi, alleging the company's 2025 Adjusted EBITDA is inflated by approximately 90% and criticizing management for extracting $58.3M via prepaid variable forward contracts while failing to substantively address the short report's factual claims.

campaigns.ask: Allegation of accounting manipulation and financial engineering, including material misstatement of at least $312 million of unrecorded debt, inflated fair value gains, and disguised borrowings as revenue/fee income.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters alleges SoFi is a financial engineering treadmill that dilutes shareholders to fund management bonuses through GE Capital-style loan marks and Enron-esque off-balance-sheet structures. The short firm claims SoFi's true personal loan charge-off rate is ~6.1% vs. reported 2.89%, and that its Loan Platform Business is a disguised form of borrowing booked as fee income rather than a capit

campaigns.ask: Allegation that AppLovin CEO and CTO made false statements denying the company's use of persistent identifiers that violate user privacy and partner platform terms of service.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters, short AppLovin, alleges that management's denials regarding the use of persistent identifiers are demonstrably false and misleading, following the firm's initial March 27, 2025 report.

campaigns.ask: Allegation: AppLovin's e-commerce conversions are largely low-incrementality retargeting, and it is violating platform partners' terms of service by collecting/structuring user IDs, risking deplatforming and competitive
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short AppLovin, alleging ~52% of APP's e-commerce conversions are retargeting with only ~25-35% incrementality, and that APP appears to violate key platform TOS by harvesting user IDs, risking deplatforming and client churn.

campaigns.ask: Allegation that FTAI violated U.S. OFAC sanctions by supplying aircraft engine parts to an Iranian MRO entity.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters presents photographic and video evidence from LinkedIn posts by an Iranian airline executive showing CFM-56 engines and FTAI packaging at Sorena Turbine in Iran, suggesting FTAI's Aerospace Products business supplied parts in violation of U.S. sanctions. The short firm notes that OFAC violations carry strict liability and potentially severe penalties, including prison time.

campaigns.ask: Allegation: Misleading financial reporting by classifying one-time whole engine sales as MRO revenue to inflate valuation.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short FTAI Aviation, alleging the company misleads investors by classifying one-time whole engine sales as Maintenance Repair & Overhaul (MRO) revenue to artificially inflate its valuation. The short seller estimates ~80% of the Aerospace Products segment's adjusted EBITDA is gains on these asset sales, which Fortress capitalized on via a May 2024 secondary offering.

campaigns.ask: Allegation that ELF materially overstated revenue by ~$135M–$190M over the past three quarters and inflated inventory, including deceiving its auditor.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short ELF, alleging the company began inflating revenue and profits in Q2 FY24 as inventory built and its growth narrative faltered. The firm contends ELF's claim that a sourcing-practice change caused a $36.9M inventory jump is false, and that the real cause was weak sales—indicating potential auditor deception.
